title: mkdir /var/run/netns permission denied
body:
/kind bug

Podman shows mkdir /var/run/netns permission denied when setting up network in bridge mode when running rootless.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. podman run -ti --log-level=debug --net=bridge node:lts bash

**Describe the results you received:**
ERRO[0005] error creating network namespace for container 2c812aa7fb6669c66df5435fe6485216b9089410c30aa64b2897d80542628752: open /var/run/netns/cni-551e8223-9974-f73b-ad61-1b908ac79e03: permission denied

**Describe the results you expected:**
container created on bridged network

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Command finished when running with sudo

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.3.0-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.4
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: To confirm - running as root, or rootless?

@giuseppe I think we're trying to use the CNI plugins here. I'm amazed it's actually gotten this far without a segfault if that's the case, given we don't even initialize the plugins for rootless. We should probably add some form of error on attempting this.

@marcino239 We don't have the privileges to set up full bridged networking when running without root, hence the default mode of `slirp4netns`. There are solutions that can offer partial control of bridging without root (LXC, for example, has a setuid binary that can do this), but we haven't integrated them into Podman as of yet.
@mheon - yes I was running rootless trying to setup bridge network.  Thanks for explanation
